Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 4, 152 [Sakikku 33][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] GAR-šu₂ GIM um-me-di a-šu-u₂ MU-⸢NI⸣

(o 1) [...]'s appearance is like an ummedu-lesion: its name is ašû-disease.

o 22

[*] ⸢GIG⸣ GAR-šu₂ GIM um-me-di i-raš-ši-šum-ma uk-kak IGI GIG A ŠUB [...] a-šu-u₂ MU-NI

(o 2) (If) a sore's appearance is like an ummedu-lesion, it is reddish and itches a lot (and) the sore's surface produces liquid [...]: its name is ašû-disease.

o 33

[*] ⸢GIG⸣ GAR-šu₂ GIM um-me-di u E₃-su e-mid SU-šu₂ i-raš-ši-šum-ma EN x [...] x TE GIG ug-dal-lab a-⸢šu⸣-[u₂ ...]

(o 3) (If) a sore's appearance is like an ummedu-lesion and what comes out of it is adhered (to it), his body is reddish and the side of the sore has been scraped until ... [...] ...: [...] ašû-disease.

o 44

[* pa]-nu-šu₂ u IGI-MIN-šu₂ SAR-MEŠḫa di-ig-la ka-bit u SA₅ a-šu-u₂ MU-NI

(o 4) (If) his face and his eyes are swollen, his vision is difficult and red: its name is ašû-disease.

o 55

[* pa]-nu-šu₂ SAR-MEŠḫu di-gil₂-šu₂ ma-ṭi SU-šu₂ bir-di ma-lu ŠA₃-šu₂ DAB-su a-šu-u₂ MU-NI

(o 5) (If) his face is swollen, his vision is diminished, his body is full of pockmarks (and) his belly affects him: its name is ašû-disease.

o 66

[*] GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM ni-šik UḪ.MA pa-gar-šu₂ SA₅ a-šu-u₂ ⸢mut-tap⸣-ri-šu MU-NI

(o 6) (If) a sore's appearance is like an insect bite (and) his body is full (of them): its name is winged ašû.

o 77

[*] GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM ter-ke-e-ti kal SU LU₂ SA₅ kul-la-ri MU-NI

(o 7) (If) a sore's appearance is like a dark spot (and) the man's entire body is full (of them): its name is kullaru-disease.

o 88

[... GAR]-šu₂ GE₆ ri-šik-<tu₂?> i-raš-ši-šum-ma uk-kak kul-la-ri MU-NI

(o 8) (If) a [...] appearance is dark, (like) desiccation, reddish (and) itches a lot: its name is kullaru-disease.

o 99

[...] ⸢GAR⸣-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ šag-gu₅ i-ta-ti-[šu₂?] um-me-di SA₅ u I [...] UN kul-la-ar₂ a-ši-i MU-NI

(o 9) (If) a [...] appearance is (that) its surface is stiff, its edges are full of ummedu-lesion and ... [...] ...: its name is kullaru of ašû-disease.

o 1010

[...] ⸢GAR⸣-šu₂ GIM um-me-di MURU₂-MEŠ-šu₂ NIGINmi ek-[ke]-tu₄ MU-NI

(o 10) (If) a [...] appearance is like an ummedu-lesion (that) surrounds his hips: its name is ekketu-disease (itching).

o 1111

[...] ⸢GAR⸣-šu₂ GIM BAR KU₆ SU-šu₂ SA₅ u a-dan-na TUKU ri-[šik]-tu₄ MU-NI

(o 11) (If) a [...] appearance is as if his body was covered with fish-scales and he is affected at a fixed date: its name is rišiktu-disease (desiccation).

o 1212

[...] ⸢GAR⸣-šu₂ GIM TAB KUM₂-ma x x GIG ri-[šu]-⸢tu₄⸣ MU-NI

(o 12) (If) a [...] appearance is as if he was hot from burning and he is sick ...: its name is rišûtu-disease (desiccation).

o 1313

[...] ⸢GAR⸣-šu₂ GIM TAB KUM₂-ma DU x A la₃ u₂-kal ⸢GIG⸣ [...] TUKU gir-giš-šu₁₄ MU-NI

(o 13) (If) a [...] appearance is as if he was hot from burning and ... does not contain fluid (and) the sore is affected by [...]: its name is girgiššu-disease.

o 1414

[*] ⸢GIG⸣ GAR-šu₂ GIM TAB KUM₂-ma A u₂-kal [bu]-bu-uʾ-tu₄ MU-NI

(o 14) (If) a sore's appearance is as if he was hot from burning and (the sore) contains liquid: its name is bubuʾtu-disease.

o 1515

[*] GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM TAB KUM₂-ma A la₃ u₂-kal U₃.⸢BU⸣.[BU].UL TUR-MEŠ SA₅ i-ši-tu₄ MU-[NI]

(o 15) (If) a sore's appearance is as if he was hot from burning and (the sore) does not contains liquid (and) is full with small bubuʾtu-boils: its name is ešītu-disease (confusion).

o 1616

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM TAB KUM₂-ma um-me-di SA₅ ri-šu-tu₂ TUKU ni-piš-tu₄ MU-[NI]

(o 16) (If) a sore's appearance is as if he was hot from burning and was affected by rišûtu-disease: its name is nipištu-disease.

o 1717

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM um-me-di u * E₃-su SAMAG DABbat ni-piš-tu₄ MU-[NI]

(o 17) (If) a sore's appearance is like an ummedu-lesion and a mole blocks what comes out of it: its name is nipištu-disease.

o 1818

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM um-me-di ig-gig i-na [...] A DU-MEŠ ru-ṭib-tu₄ MU-[NI]

(o 18) (If) the sore's appearance is like an ummedi-lesion, it itches (and) liquid flows from [...]: [its] name is ruṭibtu-disease (dropsy).

o 1919

* GIG GAR-šu₂ SA₅ BABBAR GU₇-šu₂ A u₂-⸢kal⸣ ru-ṭib-tu₄ MU-[NI]

(o 19) (If) the sore's appearance is red (or) white, it hurts him (and) contains liquid: [its] name is ruṭibtu-disease (dropsy).

o 2020

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GE₆ ḫa-ra-su MU-[NI]

(o 20) (If) a sore's appearance is black: [its] name is harāsu-disease (itching).

o 2121

* SU LU₂ bir-di SA₅ UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ u₂-zaq-qa-⸢tu-šu₂⸣ u ri-šu-tu₄ ŠUB.ŠUB-su ḫa-ra-su ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(o 21) (If) a body's man is full of pockmarks, his flesh stings him and rišûtu-disease continually falls on him: [its] name is harāsu-disease (itching).

o 2222

* GIG ina GIR₃ LU₂ lu ina ⸢ŠIR⸣ LU₂ E₁₁-⸢ma⸣ [i]-⸢raš⸣-ši-šum-ma uk-kak ru-ṭib-tu₄ ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(o 22) (If) a sore comes out on a man's foot or a man's testicle and is reddish (and) scratches: [its] name is ruṭibtu-disease (dropsy).

o 2323

* GIG GAR-šu₂ SA₅ e-em MU₂⸢iḫ⸣ u DU⸢ak⸣ sa-ma-nu [...]

(o 23) (If) a sore's appearance is red, hot, swollen and flows (liquid): [...] samānu-disease.

o 2424

* GIG GAR-šu₂ SA₅ LU₂ KUM₂.KUM₂im u i-⸢ta-nar⸣-[ru₃] sa-ma-nu [...]

(o 24) (If) the sore's appearance is red, the man is continually feverish and continually vomits: [...] samānu-disease.

o 2525

* GIG GAR-šu₂ da-an TAB x-ma [...] x x ⸢mid⸣-ru KI.MIN sa-ma-nu [...]

(o 25) (If) the sore's appearance is strong, ... burning and [...] respond to [...]: [...] midru-disease, alternatively samānu-disease.

o 2626

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM U₃.⸢BU⸣.[BU.UL ...] ši-biṭ TU₁₅ [...]

(o 26) (If) the sore's appearance is like a bubuʾtu-boil [...]: [...] šibit šāri (gust of wind).

o 2727

* GIG GAR-⸢šu₂ GIM⸣ x ⸢SA₅⸣ x [...] pe-en-tu₄ ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(o 27) (If) the sore's appearance is like like red ... [...]: [...] pendû-disease (mark on skin).

o 2828

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM na₄⸢ZU₂ TA GU₂⸣-[šu₂ ...] ša-da-nu ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(o 28) (If) a sore's appearance is like obsidian, [...] from his neck [...]: its name is šadānu-disease.

o 2929

* GIG GAR-šu₂ ana TAG da-an ti-ik-⸢pi⸣ [...] ša-da-nu ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(o 29) (If) a sore's appearance is strong to touch (and) [...] spots: its name is šadānu-disease.

o 3030

* GIG GAR-šu₂ ana TAG da-an ⸢KUM₂im⸣ ṣa-ri-⸢iḫ⸣ [...] ⸢IL₂⸣šu₂ NINDA u ⸢KAŠ⸣ LA₂ṭi ša-da-nu MU-NI TAG d[...]

(o 30) (If) a sore's appearance is strong to touch, (the patient) is feverish and (the sore) is hot, [...] are puffy (and his appetite) for bread and beer is diminished: its name is šadānu-disease. Affliction of the god [...].

o 3131

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM NA₄ da-an qer-be₂-nu-um-ma ⸢GAL⸣ [qer-be₂]-nu-um-ma DUak IL₂-šu₂ ⸢ZI⸣a [x] DU x [...]  ; la i-le-ʾ-e ša-da-nu ⸢MU⸣-NI ⸢TAG⸣ dAMAR.⸢UTU⸣ u [...]

(o 31) (If) a sore's appearance is as if it was strong like a stone, it has grown and spread inside (the body and) he cannot lift himself to stand up [...] walk: its name is šadānu-disease. Affliction of Marduk and [...].

o 3232

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM NA₄ da-an lu ina GU₂-šu₂ lu ina su-⸢ḫa⸣-ti-šu₂ lu ina ri-bi-ti-šu₂ GAR ana U₄ 3-KAM [...]

(o 32) (If) a sore's appearance is as if it was strong like a stone (and) it is located either on his neck or in his armpit or on his ribītu for 3 days [...] .

o 3333

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM KU₅is UDU.NITA₂ UŠ₂ ⸢ṣar?⸣-ḫa ⸢u₂-še⸣-ṣi GI DU₃ A GUL ⸢LU⸣ SU ana TI-šu₂ x E x x [...]

(o 33) (If) a sore's appearance is like a sheep's carcass, it expels hot blood ...: to cure him ... [...].

o 3434

* GIG GAR-šu₂ ina ši-mi-ti ŠUB-su-ma la u₂-ta-⸢šir₃⸣ x šim-ma-tu₄ ka-la pag-ri-šu₂ ⸢ṣa⸣-ba-tu x [...]

(o 34) (If) a illness's appearance is (that) it falls on him in the evening and does not leave (him) ... paralysis afflicting all his body ... [...].

o 3535

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM la i-du-u₂ di-ik-ša₂ TUKU A.ZU x x ⸢KI?⸣ U₂ la i-de a-ḫa-a-a x x x [...]

(o 35) (If) a illness's appearance is as if he no longer knew, he was affected by swelling, ... he did not know, ... my side [...].

o 3636

* ina la-ku-ti-<šu₂> la x-ma GIM x x [...] x ep-qe₂-nu MU-NI

(o 36) (If) he does not ... in his weakness and like ... [...] ...: its name is epqēnu-disease.

o 3737

* ina la-ku-⸢ti⸣-šu₂ la x-ma GIM x [x] x x MA U ZI GIŠ [...] MU-NI

(o 37) (If) he does not ... in his weakness and like .... [...] ...: its name is [...].

o 3838

* GIG GAR-šu₂ GIM im⸢ṭe₄?-ru?⸣-ti-šu₂ NIGIN x (x) u ti-ik-ka-šu₂ DAB [...] MU-NI

(o 38) (If) a sore's appearance is as if it was surrounded by its mud ... and it afflicts his neck: its name is [...].

o 3939

* GIG GAR-šu₂ da-an pa-an UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ GAR u ⸢KUM₂⸣.KUM₂im ⸢kir-ba⸣-nu MU-NI

(o 39) (If) a sore's appearance is strong, it is located on the surface of the flesh and it is continually hot: its name is kirbānu-disease.

o 4040

* GIG GAR-šu₂ kub-bu-ut u UGU-šu₂ ḫu-un-du-ud zi-iq-tu₄ MU-NI

(o 40) (If) a sore's appearance is solid and its top is deeply incised: its name is ziqtu-disease.

o 4141

[...] GAR-šu₂ GIM i-ba-⸢ri₄-ma⸣ KIR₄-šu₂ BABBAR ŠUB zi-iq-tu₄ MU-NI

(o 41) [...]'s appearance is like an ibāru-mark and its tip contains a white spot : its name is ziqtu-disease.

o 4242

[...] GAR-šu₂ SA₅ ⸢BABBAR TUR⸣ u GU₇-šu₂ zi-iq-tu₄ [MU]-NI

(o 42) [...]'s appearance is red (or) white, small and it hurts him: its name is ziqtu-disease.

o 4343

[...] x TU ŠU₂ um-mu-ra [...] TA KUR DIN? NU [... MU]-NI

(o 43) [...] ... are red [...] ...: its [name is ...].

o 4444

[... la]-⸢ku⸣-ti-šu₂ la-x-ma GU₇-⸢šu₂⸣ [...] UR x [... MU]-NI

(o 44) [...] his weakness ... and it hurts him [...] ...: its [name is ...].

o 4545

[... la]-⸢ku-ti-šu₂⸣ la-x-ma ⸢SA₅?⸣ u GU₇-šu₂ u IL₂⸢ši?⸣-šu₂ KUM₂ TUKU-MEŠ x x [... MU]-NI

(o 45) [...] his weakness ... and it is full and hurts him and puffs him up, he is continually affected by fever: its [name is ...].

o 4646

[... la-ku]-ti-šu₂ la-x-ma GU₇-šu₂ u I x LA ina ŠU BU Aʾ LAGAB [...] ⸢MU⸣-NI

(o 46) [...] his weakness ... and it hurts him and ... in ...: its name is [...].

o 4747

[... la-ku]-ti-šu₂ la-x-ma la₃ GU₇-šu₂ a-ba-bu [MU]-NI

(o 47) [...] his weakness ... and does not hurt him: its [name is] ababu-disease.

o 4848

[...] x x x-ma la₃ GU₇-šu₂!(SU) a-ba-bu [MU]-NI

(o 48) [...] ... and does not hurt him: its [name is] ababu-disease.

o 4949

[...] la₃ GU₇-šu₂!(SU) u₃ DU.DU!(UM)ak KI.MIN GUR.⸢GUR⸣ar₂ I [x] x [... MU]-NI

(o 49) [...] does not hurt him and continually spreads, alternatively it continually returns ...: its [name is ...].

o 5050

[...] ina ŠA₃-šu₂ GIM A šur-de-e-tu₄ DU-MEŠ [...] ⸢GIG la⸣-az-za [... MU]-NI

(o 50) [...] it continually flows into it like flood water [...] persistent sore [...]: its [name is ...].

o 5151

[... la-ku]-⸢ti⸣-šu₂ ⸢la⸣-x-ma GIM ⸢EN?⸣ ŠAḪ PA ZAR KASKAL x x ŠU₂ KI.MIN [...]

(o 51) [...] his weakness ... and like ... alternatively [...].

o 5252

[... la-ku]-⸢ti⸣-šu₂ ⸢la⸣-x-ma ur-ra u GE₆ GU₇-šu₂-ma la i-ṣal-lal a-bi-ik-tu₄ ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(o 52) [...] his weakness ... and it hurts him day and night so that he cannot sleep: [its] name is abiktu-disease.

o 5353

[... la-ku]-⸢ti⸣-šu₂ ⸢la⸣-x-ma GIM x x x u IL₂-šu₂ KUM₂ TUKU-MEŠ mi-iq-tu₄ MU-NI <<AŠ>> A.ZU IGI

(o 53) [...] his weakness ... and like ... and puffs him up and he is continually affected by fever: its name is miqtu-disease. He will see a physician.

o 5454

[...] x ana MAŠ.GAN₂-šu₂ GUR-ma A u₂-kal mi-iq-tu₄ MU-NI A.ZU IGI

(o 54) [...] it returns ... [...] to its (original) location and contains liquid: its name is miqtu-disease. He will see a physician.

o 5555

[...] x ana MAŠ.GAN₂-šu₂ GUR-ma la₃ ⸢TI⸣ GIG BI IN (x) x dba-ba₆ ina KI x x KI.MIN ina gišNU₂ x [...]

(o 55) [...] it returns ... [...] to its (original) location and he does not recover: that patient ... Bau on ... alternatively, in his bed ... [...].

o 5656

[...] ṣe-e-ti₃-ma SA ḪI [... sa]-⸢ḫi-pi-šu⸣ IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ GE₆ ŠUBu₂ gal-lu-u₂ MU-NI

(o 56) [...] inssuance (of abscess) and ... [...] its coating its surface contains black spots: its name is gallû-disease.

o 5757

[...] x LUGUD u₂-⸢kal⸣-ma [...]-šu₂ MU-NI

(o 57) [...] ... contains pus and [...]: its name is [...] ....

o 5858

[...] ⸢BABBAR⸣ LUGUD E₃-ma x [...] šaḫ₃-šaḫ₃-ḫu MU-NI

(o 58) [...] white [...] (and) pus come out and ... [...]: its name is šahšahhu-disease.

o 5959

[...] GU₇-šu₂ A la₃ TUKU x x i-[rap-pu]-uš NI LU GU₄ MU-NI

(o 59) [...] hurts him, (the sore) produces no liquid, ..., it spreads: its name is ...-disease.

o 6060

[... GAR]-šu₂ TAG-su-ma i-rap-⸢pu⸣-[uš] E₃-su GU₂.ZAL MU-[NI]

(o 60) [...] appearance is that he touches it so that it spreads: what which goes out is called guzallu.

o 6161

[...] TI ŠU [...] MURU₂ u uzuUR₂ il-la-a-⸢tu₂ DU-MEŠ⸣ [... GU₂].⸢ZAL⸣ MU-NI

(o 61) [...] ... [...] of hip and thigh, saliva continually flows [...]: [...] is called guzallu.

o 6262

[...] BI IL BI IR QIR BI [...] x sim-ma GIG ki-ṣir-tu₂ GIG [...] MU-NI

(o 62) [...] ... [...] he suffers from simmu-lesion, he suffers from constriction [...]: its name is [...].

o 6363

[...] NU NE DI NIR SA₅ GAR [...] KUM₂ sik-ka-tu₄ MU-NI

(o 63) [...] redness ... is located [...], it is feverish: its name is sikkatu-disease.

o 6464

[...]-šu₂ ṣa-bit u GE₆ šag-ba-ni MU-[NI]

(o 64) [...] ... it is affected and dark: its name is šagbānu-disease.

o 6565

[...] x (x) I SAG SU BI x IŠ ṣi-in-na-[aḫ]-ti-⸢ru⸣ [...]

(o 65) [...] ...: [...] ṣinnahtiru-disease (diarrhoea).

o 6666

[...] U₄ TUKU u IR u₂-⸢kal?⸣ ṣi-in-na-aḫ-ti-[ru ...]

(o 66) [...] it is affected by ... and suffers from sweat: [...] ṣinnahtiru-disease (diarrhoea).

o 6767

[...] x-MEŠ u DUak ṢI KAL x [...]

(o 67) [...] ... and flows: ... [...] ....

o 6868

[...] x x [...] ZI₂ RAB x [...]

(o 68) [...] ... [...] ... [...].

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] x [...]

(r 1) (traces)

r 22

[...] x x [...] x (x) [...]

r 33

[...] E₃a SAḪAR.ŠUB.BA [...]

(r 3) [...] comes out: leprosy [...].

r 44

[...] x MA GA GIM ⸢A⸣ [...] ⸢E₃⸣a SAḪAR.ŠUB.BA [...]

(r 4) [...] ... like ... [...] comes out: leprosy [...].

r 55

[...] SU MA BAD MEŠ [...] x ŠUL PI diš₈-tar₂-ti₃ [...]

(r 5) [...] ... [...] ... of the goddess [...]

r 66

[...] NA GUL [...] x [...]

(r 6) (traces)

r 77

[...] x GIG BI E BI [...] ⸢DINGIR⸣ IRI

(r 7) [...] that patient ... [...] city god.

r 88

[...] x KI.MIN ṣa-lam ZA RI IG x [...] mut-tal-lik SILA KI.MIN ŠU di-šum GAL LA [...]

(r 8) [...] ..., alternatively image of ... [...] roamer of the street, alternatively hand of Išum ... [...].

r 99

[...] x DIR [...] ⸢d⸣iš-tar ŠU diš-ḫa-[ra ...]

(r 9) [...] ... [...] Ištar, hand of Išhara [...].

r 1010

[...] ŠU₂ DIR DIR [...] d[...]

(r 10) (traces)

r 1111

[...] ŠA₃-šu₂ IGI SA₅ [...] d[...]

(r 11) [...] ... its surface is red [...] the god [...].

r 1212

[...] pa-gar-šu₂ SA₅ ma-gal BABBAR KI.MIN [na]-gi-il ŠU d30 ⸢MU⸣-[NI]

(r 12) [...] his body is full of [...], it is very white, alternatively it is glistening: [its] name is hand of Sin.

r 1313

[...] ina UNU₂-šu₂ E₃ [...] de₂-[a ...]

(r 13) [...] erupts on his upper cheek: [...] Ea [...].

r 1414

[...] ina ker-ri-šu₂ u MURU₂ x [...] x NU [...]

(r 14) [...] on his collarbone and in the middle of ... [...] ... [...].

r 1515

[...] ina SAG ŠA₃-šu₂ E₃ ⸢IGI⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ KUR₂.KUR₂ru ŠU diš-⸢ḫa⸣-[ra ...]

(r 15) [...] erupts at the top of his belly (and) his face continually changes: [...] hand of Išhara.

r 1616

[...]-la-šu₂ u ši-bit ŠIR-šu₂ E₃ IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ KUR₂.KUR₂ru u SA₅ ŠU diš₈-tar₂-⸢ri⸣-šu₂

(r 16) [...] ... and grey hair emerges on his testicles, his face continually changes and is red: [...] hand of his goddess.

r 1717

[...] ŠA₃-šu₂ SIG₂ GE₆ E₃a x [...]

(r 17) [...] black hairs emerge [...] his belly ... [...].

r 1818

[...] ŠA₃-šu₂ SIG₂ BABBAR E₃a NU x [...]

(r 18) [...] white hairs emerge [...] his belly (and) not ... [...].

r 1919

[... bu-bu-uʾ]-ta SA₅ u il-la-tu-šu₂ il-⸢la⸣-ka bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu MU-⸢NI⸣

(r 19) [...] is full of bubuʾtu-boils and his saliva flows: its name is buʾšānu-disease.

r 2020

[... il-la-tu]-šu₂!(ŠA₂) UŠ₂ u₂-kal-la bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu MU-⸢NI⸣

(r 20) [...] his saliva contains blood: its name is buʾšānu-disease.

r 2121

[...] eb-ṭu u KA-šu₂ ⸢ka-bit⸣ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu MU-⸢NI⸣

(r 21) [...] are swollen and his mouth (feels) heavy: its name is buʾšānu-disease.

r 2222

[...] ⸢GABA-su⸣ u ša₂-šal-la-šu₂ KUM₂-MEŠ bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu MU-⸢NI⸣

(r 22) [...], chest and back are feverish: its name is buʾšānu-disease.

r 2323

[... ḫa-aḫ]-ḫaš u ŠA₃-MEŠ-šu₂ eb-ṭu bu-uʾ-ša₂-nu MU-⸢NI⸣

(r 23) [...] is burning with fever and his intestines are swollen: its name is buʾšānu-disease.

r 2424

[... IGI]-MEŠ-šu₂ SIG₇ šiḫ-ḫat UZU TUKU-MEŠ a-mur-⸢ri-qa⸣-nu MU-[NI]

(r 24) [...] his [eyes] are yellow-green (and) he is affected by wasting of the flesh: [its] name is amurriqānu-disease (jaundice).

r 2525

[...] ⸢SIG₇⸣ ŠA₃ IGI-MIN-šu₂ SIG₇ u SUḪUŠ EME-šu₂ GE₆ ⸢aḫ-ḫa⸣-[zu ...]

(r 25) [...] are yellow-green, the inner part of his eyes are yellow-green and the root of his tongue is black: [...] ahhāzu-disease (jaundice).

r 2626

[...] ⸢ti⸣-ik-ka-šu₂ u ša-šal!(PU-)la-šu₂ 1niš GU₇-MEŠ-⸢šu₂⸣ ša₂-aš-[ša₂-ṭu ...]

(r 26) [...] his neck and his back hurt him at the same time: [...] šaššaṭu-disease.

r 2727

[...] ⸢MURU₂-MEŠ⸣-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ aš-ṭa ša₂-aš-[ša₂-ṭu ...]

(r 27) [...] his hips, hands and feet are stiff: [...] šaššaṭu-disease.

r 2828

[... i]-zur-ma IGI-šu₂ gal-ta-at pi-qa la pi-qa i-par-ru-ud KI.MIN I x [...] ⸢ša₂-aš⸣-[ša₂-ṭu ...]

(r 28) [...] twists and his eye is jerky (and) it trembles repeatedly, alternatively ... [...]: [...] šaššaṭu-disease.

r 2929

[... SA].⸢GU₂⸣ni-šu₂ EN eq-bi-šu₂ SA-MEŠ-šu₂ šag-gu₅ šu-ʾ-ra-šu₂ kaṣ₃-ra u ⸢is⸣-[sa-šu₂] ḫe-sa-a [...]

(r 29) [...] his muscles are stiff [...] his neck to his heels, his eyebrows are knotted and his jaws are tense [...].

r 3030

[* SA]-⸢MEŠ⸣ uzuUR₂-šu₂ 1niš GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ ZIa u DU-MEŠka la i-le-ʾ-e SA.GAL [...]

(r 30) (If) his thigh muscles hurt him at the same time (and) he cannot stand up and walk: [...] sagallu-disease.

r 3131

[...] uzuUR₂-šu₂ TA giš-ši-šu₂ EN ki-ṣal-li-šu₂ GU₇-šu₂ ZIbe₂ u DUak maš-ka-⸢du₃⸣ [...]

(r 31) (If) his thigh hurts him from the hip to the ankle (but) he can stand up and walk: [...] maškadu-disease.

r 3232

[...] uzuGIŠ.KUN-šu₂ EN ŠU.SI-MEŠ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SA-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ maš-ka-⸢du₃⸣ [...]

(r 32) (If) his muscles hurt him [...] his lower back to his toes: [...] maškadu-disease.

r 3333

[... giš]-ši-šu₂ EN pi-ṭir ki-ṣil-li-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ ḫi-ṭa₄ NU TUKU ki-iṣ-ṣa-⸢tu₄⸣ [...]

(r 33) [...] hurt him [...] hip to the piṭru of his ankle, there is no harm: [...] kiṣṣatu-disease.

r 3434

[*] TA giš-ši-šu₂ EN ŠU.SI-MEŠ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SA-MEŠ-šu₂ it-te-nen-ṣi-⸢la-šu₂ ki-iṣ-sat⸣ U₄.⸢DA⸣ [...]

(r 34) (If) his muscles are continually paralysed from his hip to his toes: [...] kiṣṣat ṣēti-disease.

r 3535

sa-ma-nu [x] dGU.LA [a]-šu-u₂ ŠU d[...]

(r 35) samānu-disease: [...] of Gula. ašû-disease: hand of (the deity) [...].

r 3636

ṣi-i-tu₄ ŠU dGU.LA x [...] ŠU d[...]

(r 36) ṣītu-disease: hand of Gula. ... [...]: hand of (the deity) [...].

r 3737

ṣar-ri-šu₂ ŠU dGU.LA ⸢ša⸣-[da]-nu ŠU d⸢GU⸣.[LA]

(r 37) Spreading (of a wound): hand of Gula. šadānu-disease: hand of Gula.

r 3838

ṣi-in-na-aḫ-ti-ri ŠU dGU.LA u ⸢ša⸣-[da]-⸢nu⸣ ŠU dNIN.[URTA]

(r 38) Diarrhoea: hand of Gula, and šadānu-disease: hand of Ninurta.

r 3939

ta-kal-tu₄ ŠU dNIN.URTA aḫ-⸢ḫa-zu⸣ ŠU d[...]

(r 39) Stomach (ache): hand of Ninurta. ahhāzu-disease (jaundice): hand of (the deity) [...].

r 4040

di-ik-šu₂ ŠU dAMAR.UTU u [...] x x RA x [...]

(r 40) Bulge: hand of Marduk and [...] ... [...].

r 4141

[...]-aḫ-ḫi-iz ŠU dAMAR.UTU um-me-di ⸢ŠU⸣ de₂-[a]

(r 41) [...] ...: hand of Marduk. ummedi-lesion: hand of Ea.

r 4242

[...] ŠU dUTU gir-giš-šu₁₄ ŠU dUTU

(r 42) [...]: hand of Šamaš. girgiššu: hand of Šamaš.

r 4343

[...] ŠU dUTU ek-ke-tu₄ ŠU dUTU

(r 43) [...]: hand of Šamaš. Itching: hand of Šamaš.

r 4444

[...] ŠU dUTU qu-lip-tu₄ ŠU dUTU

(r 44) [...]: hand of Šamaš. quliptu-lesion: hand of Šamaš.

r 4545

[...] ŠU dUTU U₃.BU.BU.⸢UL⸣ BABBAR! ŠU dUTU TIuṭ

(r 45) [...]: hand of Šamaš. White bubuʾtu-boil: hand of Šamaš, he will recover.

r 4646

[...] ŠU diš-tar TAGit nam-tar ul TIuṭ U₃.BU.⸢BU.UL SA₅⸣ ŠU d30 KI.MIN

(r 46) [...]: hand of Ištar. Affliction of fate, he will not survive. Red bubuʾtu-boil: hand of Sin. Alternatively [...]: hand of Šamaš.

r 4747

[...] ⸢ŠU⸣ dUTU GIR₃ 2.30-šu₂ x [...] ŠU d⸢iš⸣-tar

(r 47) His left foot ...: hand of Ištar.

r 4848

[...] ŠU d30 a-na EGIR-šu₂ x [...] ŠU d[...] x

(r 48) [...]: hand of Sin. [...] on his backside: hand of (the deity) [...] ....

r 4949

[...] x ŠU diš-tar bu-up-pa-ni-šu₂ ⸢im⸣-[qut] ŠU d[...]

(r 49) [...] ...: hand of Ištar. (If) he falls on his face: hand of (the deity) [...].

r 5050

[...] x u ŠA₃-MEŠ-šu₂ it-te-nen-bi-ṭu UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ x [...] ⸢ŠU⸣ [...]

(r 50) [...] ... and his intestines swell, his flesh ... [...]: hand of [...].

r 5151

[...] UZU-MEŠ-šu₂ i-šaḫ-ḫu-ḫu [...] ⸢ŠU⸣ [...]

(r 51) [...] his flesh is loose [...]: hand of [...].

r 5252

[...] x AN.TA GU₇-šu₂-ma la i-ṣal-lal ⸢ŠU⸣ [dŠUL].PA.E₃.A

(r 52) [...] ... hurts him above so that he cannot sleep: hand of Šulpae.

r 5353

[...] x AN.TA GU₇-šu₂-ma la i-ṣal-lal ⸢ŠU⸣ [d]⸢iš⸣-tar

(r 53) [...] ... hurts him above so that he cannot sleep: hand of Ištar.

r 5454

[...] x GIG-[su?] uk-ku-up [...]

(r 54) [...] ... [his] illness is early [...].

r 5555

[...] x TI [...] x a-mur-ri-qa-nu ḫi-[ṭa₄ ...]

(r 55) [...] ... [...] ... jaundice. Harm [...].

(catchline)
r 5656

[...] x TI ŠA₃-šu₂ la₃ IL₂-šu₂ MUNUS BI ŠA₃-[šu₂ ...]

(r 56) [...] ... has no desire, that woman [...] desire [...].

(colophon)
r 5757

[x SUMUN]-šu₂ SAR-ma ba-ru₃ ṭup-pi mri-mut-d[a-nu ...]

(r 57) Written and checked [...] its original. Tablet of Rimut-Anu, [...] of Šangi-Ninurta, incantation priest, Urukean.

r 5858

[... lu₂]E₂.MAŠ—<dNIN.URTA> lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ lu₂qaq-qar-[da-nuki-u₂]

r 5959

[...] la ⸢i-tab-bal⸣ [...]

(r 59) [...] shall not take (it) away [...].
